learning_outcomes.description: Explain the geotechnical design process and the challenges faced by geotechnical engineers in designing civil infrastructure.
Evaluate the lateral earth pressures at failure for various types of soils and groundwater conditions.
Explain how limit analysis methods are employed in designing geotechnical structures.
Apply elasticity theory to geotechnical design problems and understand their limitations.
Design gravity and embedded retaining walls in accordance with accepted engineering practice and standards.
Evaluate the ultimate bearing capacity and expected settlement of shallow footings.
Assess the axial and lateral capacity and deformation of deep footings.
Assess the stability of natural and man-made slopes.
Understand and describe basic soil behaviour related to geotechnical problems including retaining walls, shallow and deep footings, and slope stability while collaborating in a team.

assessments.task_description:
Students will work in pairs to conduct one of five experiments during the session. Each pair will create a 7-minute video documenting their experiment, which will include an audio explanation of the objectives, methodology, and findings. Additionally, they will explain the other experiments they have not conducted themselves and answer pre-set questions related to both their experiment and the others. This assessment ensures a comprehensive understanding of their own work and the overall laboratory context.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. 


A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission of video file using the provided link on Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Retaining Wall (5%) 27/03/2026 12:00 pm

Shallow Footing (5%) 1/05/2026 12:00 pm

Pile Foundations (5%) 15/05/2026 12:00 pm

Slope Stability (5%) 29/05/2026 12:00 pm

Quiz will be available for 24 hours (from Thursday 12:00 to Friday 12:00) to be completed within one (1) hour.
assessments.task_description:
These quizzes test your understanding of various concepts in geotechnical engineering. Short answer and calculation-based questions.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. 

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Exams submitted after the end of the submission time will incur a late penalty.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
